what_it_does: A fast-coverage inventory note (Region D, 2026-06-08, read-only) mapping the Company codebase's self-description layer (AGENTS.md/MAP.md/STATE.md), tests/ (130 acceptance suites), docs/, and ops/ — with directory trees, priority-tagged tables, a full categorized listing of all 130 test suites, summary counts, a top-10 deep-read list, key insights, and gaps.
observations: Lists 16 modules WITH AGENTS.md and 2 directories WITHOUT (build-prep/, design/), declaring both as non-code.
A self-description table maps each AGENTS.md to its module, the contracts it governs (C1-C8, S1/S5/S6/S7, D3, C2.1-C2.5), and a one-line characterization.

Enumerates all 130 acceptance test suites by hand, grouped into ~13 categories (Engine E1-E6, Wire S8, Address Grammar C3, Conversation, RHM, Activation, Governance/Self-Mod, Fabric, Voice, Data, Presentation, Misc).
Records concrete ops detail: gpu.py VRAM fit-gate measured on a 16GB card (4B brain solo 256K context; co-resides with light voices at 64K; Orpheus ~8.5GB blocks).
Names two anchoring design decisions: D3 (one substrate, per-type view-modes) and PoLR-3 (path-of-least-resistance self-maintenance).
States MAP.md is self-maintained by Suite.refresh_self_description() and STATE.md drift is checked by tests/drift_acceptance.py.
Includes a run command for the suite: for t in tests/*.py; do ./.venv/bin/python "$t"; done.
Notes ext4-only storage constraint (no /mnt/c due to WSL fsync corruption) for the store module.
trajectory_acceptance.py appears twice in the listing (lines 208 and 251).
standouts: The complete hand-enumerated catalog of all 130 acceptance suites with a one-line purpose each — a full proof-record index.
The D3 framing that the ops console is the FIRST instantiation of 'one substrate, per-type view-modes', with cognitive/RHM/data/jobs as pending view-modes of the same mechanism.
The wire_adversarial scope-overrun rule: a build touching paths outside declared scope is NOT closed.
The claim 'tests ARE the record' — done = green suite + use-evidence, never code-read-only, enforced by drift-check.
conventions: Uses priority-tagged (YES/NO/Maybe) tables as the inventory backbone.
Cross-references the codebase's numbered contract/spec codes rather than redefining them.
Follows the repo's self-description discipline (every code module has an AGENTS.md, single MAP/STATE at root).
concerns: trajectory_acceptance.py is listed twice, so the hand-counted '130' may not exactly match the actual file count.
Several VRAM/context figures are stated as measured but the note is explicitly coverage-level and read-only, so they are reported, not verified here.
notes: A 2026-06-08 read-only map of the self-description / tests / docs / ops regions, with a complete 130-suite catalog and the D3 + PoLR-3 design anchors — a navigation and proof-record index.
questions: Is the duplicate trajectory_acceptance.py entry a listing error, and does the actual suite count equal 130?
What do the contract codes (C1-C8, S1-S8, D3) resolve to in build-prep/contracts/?
Are the pending ops view-modes (cognitive/RHM/data/jobs) still pending as of now, or have they since been instantiated?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — it declares itself a fast coverage read-only scan of Region D and delivers exactly that, with explicit gaps/pending caveats.
apparent_themes: The system describes itself (auto-maintained registries, drift-fails-loud)
Tests as the proof record / done = green + use-evidence
intention_signals: Aims to give a refresh agent a single map of the meta layer — where the constitution, proofs, docs, and operational control live, what governs what, and which files anchor the whole — so navigation does not require re-discovery.
novelty: The full one-line annotation of 130 acceptance suites in a single note, plus the explicit linkage of each AGENTS.md to its governing contract codes.
contribution: Contributes a complete catalog of the 130 acceptance suites, the module-to-contract governance map, and the operational-detail (VRAM fit-gate, capability registry, systemd substrate) plus the D3/PoLR-3 design anchors that explain why the layer is shaped as it is.
summary_for_embedding: A read-only coverage inventory mapping the Company codebase's self-description files, all 130 acceptance test suites by category, docs, and ops/CLI, with priority tables and the D3 and PoLR-3 design anchors.
intention_for_embedding: To give a refresh agent a navigable map of the meta layer — constitution, proof record, docs, and operational control — and the design decisions that shape it.
